title: lora_l2.c: TX copies packet twice unnecessarily
description:
In lichen_lora_l2_tx() (lora_l2.c:263-265), the data is copied into a stack buffer tx_buf[256] only because Zephyr's lora_send() takes a non-const pointer. However, the caller lichen_l2_send() already holds a mutex and uses its own tx_frame_buf[].

What goes wrong: Every TX pays ~200 bytes of memcpy + 256 bytes of stack pressure. At SF10 the radio is the bottleneck, but on memory-constrained boards the stack could fragment or hit limits during deep call stacks.

Fix: Pass tx_frame_buf directly from lichen_l2_send() to lora_send() by removing the const from lichen_lora_l2_tx() signature. The caller owns the buffer under mutex, so mutation is safe.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:263-265
